title: Erdős-Rado Sunflower Conjecture
statement_text: There is a constant $C = C(r)$ depending only on $r$ such that $f_r(k) \leq C^k$.
context_text: Erdős and Rado proved in 1960 that $f_r(k) \leq k!(r-1)^k$, where $f_r(k)$ is the minimum size of a family of $k$-sets that must contain an $r$-sunflower. The conjecture asserts the correct bound is purely exponential in $k$. The paper studies this conjecture for families of bounded VC-dimension, bounded Littlestone dimension, and geometrically defined set systems.
paper_contribution: Verifies the conjecture for families of bounded Littlestone dimension (proving $h^d_r(k) \leq (rk)^d$), for families with VC-dimension 1 (proving $f^1_r(k) \leq r^{10k}$), and for pseudo-disk families; proves the near-exponential bound $f^d_r(k) \leq 2^{10k(dr)^2 \log^* k}$ for families of bounded VC-dimension $d \geq 2$.
notes: PDF source — minor math garbling in surrounding text but the conjecture statement itself is unambiguous.
paper_title: Sunflowers in set systems of bounded dimension

title: Conjecture 1.1
statement_text: For $d \geq 1$ and $r \geq 3$, there is a constant $C = C(d, r)$ such that $f^d_r(k) \leq C^k$.
context_text: The authors note that the Erdős-Rado sunflower conjecture implies this weaker statement about families of bounded VC-dimension $d$, where $f^d_r(k)$ is the minimum family size guaranteeing an $r$-sunflower among $k$-sets with VC-dimension at most $d$. The paper proves the conjecture for $d = 1$ (Theorem 1.2) and establishes the slightly weaker bound $f^d_r(k) \leq 2^{10k(dr)^2 \log^* k}$ for $d \geq 2$ (Theorem 1.3), leaving the full conjecture open for $d \geq 2$.
notes: PDF source. Conjecture is explicitly labelled in the paper. The case $d=1$ is resolved by Theorem 1.2; the case $d \geq 2$ remains open.
paper_title: Sunflowers in set systems of bounded dimension
